title: GETTING STARTED — taint
text:
Update node 'foo' with a taint with key 'dedicated' and value 'special-user' and effect 'NoSchedule' # If a taint with that key and effect already exists, its value is replaced as specified
kubectl taint nodes foo
dedicated
=special-user:NoSchedule
Remove from node 'foo' the taint with key 'dedicated' and effect 'NoSchedule' if one exists
kubectl
taint
nodes
foo
dedicated
:NoSchedule-
Remove from node 'foo' all the taints with key 'dedicated'
kubectl
taint nodes foo dedicated-
Add a taint with key 'dedicated' on nodes having label mylabel=X
kubectl taint
node
-l
myLabel=
X
dedicated=
foo:PreferNoSchedule
Add to node 'foo' a taint with key 'bar' and no value
kubectl
taint
nodes
foo
bar
:NoSchedule
Update the taints on one or more nodes.
A taint consists of a key, value, and effect. As an argument here, it is expressed as key=value:effect.
The key must begin with a letter or number, and may contain letters, numbers, hyphens, dots, and underscores, up to 253 characters.
Optionally, the key can begin with a DNS subdomain prefix and a single '/', like example.com/my-app.
The value is optional. If given, it must begin with a letter or number, and may contain letters, numbers, hyphens, dots, and underscores, up to 63 characters.
The effect must be NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule or NoExecute.
Currently taint can only apply to node.
